qtype: percentage increase, ratio, standard form, percentage of quantity
tex:
A company employed 300 workers when it started and now employs 852 workers.
\newline

(a) 
Calculate the percentage increase in the number of workers.
\newline

 \%

Of the 852 workers, the ratio part-time workers : full-time workers = 5 : 7.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the number of full-time workers.
\newline

The company makes 40 600 headphones in one year.
\newline
 \\
Write this number

(i)  in words,

 in standard form.
\newline

In one month, the company sells 3000 headphones.
\newline
 \\
Of these, 48\% are exported, $\frac{3}{8}$ are sold to shops and the rest are sold online.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the number of headphones that are sold online.
\newline

One year, sales increased by 15\%.
\newline
 \\
The following year sales increased by 18\%.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the overall percentage increase in sales.
\newline

 \%

content.content:
Calculate the percentage increase in the number of workers.
\newline

............................................. \%

\newline

............................................. \%

qtype: limits of accuracy, upper bound, percentage decrease, reverse percentage, currency conversion
tex:
Two rectangular picture frames are mathematically similar.
\newline

(a) 
The areas of the frames are 350 cm$^2$ and 1134 cm$^2$.
\newline
 \\
The width of the smaller frame is 17.5 cm.
\newline

Calculate the width of the larger frame.
\newline

A picture in the smaller frame has length 15 cm and width 10.5 cm, both correct to the nearest 5 mm.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the area of this picture.
\newline

In a sale, the price of a large frame is reduced by 18\%.
\newline
 \\
Parthi pays \$166.05 for 5 large frames in the sale.
\newline

Calculate the original price of one large frame.
\newline

\$ 

Parthi advertises a large frame for a price of \$57 or 48.20 euros.
\newline
 \\
The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.88 euros.
\newline

Calculate the difference between these prices, in dollars and cents, correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\$

content.content:
The areas of the frames are 350 cm$^2$ and 1134 cm$^2$.
\newline
 \\
The width of the smaller frame is 17.5 cm.
\newline

Calculate the width of the larger frame.
\newline

A picture in the smaller frame has length 15 cm and width 10.5 cm, both correct to the nearest 5 mm.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the area of this picture.
\newline

In a sale, the price of a large frame is reduced by 18\%.
\newline
 \\
Parthi pays \$166.05 for 5 large frames in the sale.
\newline

Calculate the original price of one large frame.
\newline

\$ ................................................

Parthi advertises a large frame for a price of \$57 or 48.20 euros.
\newline
 \\
The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.88 euros.
\newline

Calculate the difference between these prices, in dollars and cents, correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\$ ................................................

stem.content:
Two rectangular picture frames are mathematically similar.
\newline

_rawBody:
Two rectangular picture frames are mathematically similar.

\begin{parts}
\part The areas of the frames are 350 cm$^2$ and 1134 cm$^2$. \\
The width of the smaller frame is 17.5 cm.

Calculate the width of the larger frame.

\AnswerLine[][cm]
\Marks{3}


\part A picture in the smaller frame has length 15 cm and width 10.5 cm, both correct to the nearest 5 mm.

Calculate the upper bound for the area of this picture.

\AnswerLine[][cm$^2$]
\Marks{2}


\part In a sale, the price of a large frame is reduced by 18\%. \\
Parthi pays \$166.05 for 5 large frames in the sale.

Calculate the original price of one large frame.

\$ ................................................
\Marks{2}


\part Parthi advertises a large frame for a price of \$57 or 48.20 euros. \\
The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.88 euros.

Calculate the difference between these prices, in dollars and cents, correct to the nearest cent.

\$ ................................................
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

qtype: forming and solving quadratic equations from word problem, algebraic manipulation
tex:
Darpan runs a distance of 12 km and then cycles a distance of 26 km.
\newline
 \\
His running speed is $x$ km/h and his cycling speed is 10 km/h faster than his running speed.
\newline
 \\
He takes a total time of 2 hours 48 minutes.
\newline

(a) 
An expression for the time, in hours, Darpan takes to run the 12 km is $\frac{12}{x}$.
\newline
 \\
Write an equation, in terms of $x$, for the total time he takes in hours.
\newline

Show that this equation simplifies to $7x^2 - 25x - 300 = 0$.
\newline

Use the quadratic formula to solve $7x^2 - 25x - 300 = 0$.
\newline
 \\
You must show all your working.
\newline

x =  or x = 

Calculate the number of minutes Darpan takes to run the 12 km.
\newline

 min

\newline

x = ................... or x = ..................

 \\

tex:

\newline

\newline

 Find $n((T \cup (G \cup M))')$.

\newline
 \\
Find the probability that this student wears trainers but does not wear glasses.
\newline

\newline
 \\
Find the probability that both students have mobile phones.
\newline
